title: nOS Tier-2 App Manifest (apps/<name>.yml)
description: Coolify-style lightweight app manifest. Validated by module_utils/nos_app_parser.py and consumed by pazny.apps_runner. The `gdpr:` block is mandatory — apps cannot deploy without a complete GDPR Article 30 record. See docs/framework-overview.md for two-tier rationale, apps/_template.yml for a worked example.

name.description: App slug; matches the filename without .yml.

version.description: Upstream image tag pinned for this manifest.

gdpr.description: GDPR Article 30 register entry. EVERY field required. The parser refuses to deploy without this block.

purpose.description: Plain-language sentence: why we process this data.

legal_basis.description: GDPR Art 6(1) lawful basis. `consent` triggers the SSO gate.

data_categories.description: Categories of personal data processed, e.g. ['email','name','ip_address','photos'].

data_subjects.description: Who the subjects are. Values from {end_users, employees, partners, patients, minors, anonymous} drive the TLS gate.

retention_days.description: Auto-erasure horizon in days. Use -1 for explicit 'forever' (audit-only). 0 is invalid (use -1 if intentional).

processors.description: Third-party processors. Empty list OK but explicit. Each entry should be a name + URL or a sub-record.

transfers_outside_eu.description: True = data leaves the EU/EEA (triggers the EU-residency registry gate to be bypassed; operator acknowledges).

dsar_endpoint.description: Optional shell command or HTTP endpoint the DSAR worker invokes on erasure requests.

breach_severity_default.description: Default severity tag for incidents in Wing's breach register.

services.description: Verbatim docker-compose `services:` block; magic tokens resolved by the runner.

nginx.description: Optional vhost auto-derivation hints. If absent the runner still creates a default vhost from meta.name + meta.ports[0].

extra_directives.description: Raw nginx config snippet appended inside `location /`.

depends_on.description: Other Tier-1 / Tier-2 service names this app needs running first. The runner reorders the apps_up loop to honour this.
